insight: For Türkiye, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have been a significant factor in gaining momentum, with the team successfully doing so in both of their breaks, resulting in a mean increase of 0.100 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have given Türkiye an opportunity to regroup and refocus, leading to improved performance on the field. The data also indicates that when Türkiye was not pressing before a break, they tended to gain more momentum afterwards, with an average increase of 0.200 points per minute, implying that the team is able to capitalize on the break to adjust their strategy and come out stronger. Overall, the hydration breaks have had a positive impact on Türkiye's gameplay, allowing them to build momentum and potentially change the course of a match.
